summary: Joel Spolsky announces a new wiki dedicated to collecting information about the business of software. The post is extremely brief, essentially just linking to the wiki and quoting its mission statement. In a characteristically playful move, Joel demonstrates the wiki's editability by correcting the awkward phrasing of the wiki's own description in real time. The post serves as both an announcement and a live demonstration of how wikis work.
key_points: A new wiki has been created to consolidate information about the business of software across all segments, from microISVs to enterprise
Joel demonstrates the wiki concept by editing the wiki's own description to improve its phrasing
The post doubles as an invitation for readers to contribute by editing the wiki themselves
money_quotes: The point of this wiki is to bring under one roof as much useful, quality information as possible about the business of software, whether it's microISVs selling desktop software, Web 2.0 sites or even the big enterprise kind of outfits.
Hmm, gee, that phrasing is a little bit awkward.
key_insight: The best way to demonstrate a collaborative tool is to use it live, turning an announcement into a participatory invitation.
